title: Deterministic Telegram-origin routing for post-restart completion
summary: Persist Telegram identity metadata in restart context and restore it during boot routing so restart completions always return to the correct Telegram session/channel.

affectedFiles.description: Add optional Telegram identity fields to RestartContext and persist them when saving restart context.
Resolve deterministic post-restart routing target and restore Telegram origin/identity for Telegram-triggered restarts.

diffPreview:
lifecycle.ts: RestartContext gains previousTelegramChatId?: string and previousTelegramUserId?: number; persisted restart context now captures telegramChatId/telegramUserId from sessionContext.
boot.ts: import SESSION_ORIGIN_TELEGRAM; replace telegram/non-telegram branching with resolvedChannel/resolvedOrigin/resolvedSessionId defaults (telegram-bootstrap for telegram without previous session); before routing log, force Telegram origin + restore telegramChatId/telegramUserId when context indicates Telegram.
estimatedImpact: Prevents wrong-channel restart confirmations, ensures deterministic Telegram completion routing, and improves operator trust with minimal scoped risk.
executorPrompt: Apply the exact edits described in this proposal to src/gateway/lifecycle.ts and src/gateway/boot.ts. Preserve formatting and existing logic outside the specified blocks. Then run npm run build and report success/failure with any compiler output. If build succeeds, summarize changed lines and confirm deterministic Telegram routing behavior matches the proposal.
